content.method: Regional pilot with staggered rollout (stepped-wedge design) comparing tier assignment patterns, mainstream inclusion rates, and pre/post reclassification flows against matched non-pilot regions; track whether prior top-tier or unclassified students shift into the new middle tier using linked administrative records, mirroring Finland's post-2010 tier-flow analysis.
content.question: If a Finland-style tiered support model were introduced in Hungary, would the new middle tier capture genuinely unmet need, or would it primarily relabel/reclassify students already served in mainstream or top-tier settings, and would county-level disparities persist under the new architecture?
content.why_it_matters: This determines whether the most structurally ambitious proposal (T3) delivers real capacity gains or merely relocates existing classification volatility, which is decision-critical given the high implementation risk and legal-reform burden T3 entails.
